channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Back-to-back earthquakes jolt Los Angeles area within hours
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 3.5 rattled the L.A. metropolitan area early Saturday just hours after a 3.8-magnitude quake shook the Malibu area shortly before midnight Friday.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: Rodeo rider cheats death when bull's horn slices neck open
item.description: A fearless rodeo rider in Florida had a close brush with death when the bull he was riding gored him in the neck while being bucked off the beast’s back.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 20% down? The myth that could be holding home buyers back
item.description: Scraping together a down payment for a home is a challenge, especially in today’s housing market. But one persistent financial myth could be making it harder than it needs to be.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, Fox 5

item.title: Trump moves with dizzying speed on his to-do list. But there are warning signs in his first month
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump approaches the first-month mark in his second term, he has moved with dizzying speed and blunt force to reorder American social and political norms and the economy while redefining the U.S. role in the world.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, JOSH BOAK and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel and Hamas complete their latest exchange as ceasefire's first phase has just 2 weeks left
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel and Hamas completed the sixth exchange of hostages and hundreds of Palestinian prisoners on Saturday with just over two weeks remaining in the initial phase of their fragile Gaza ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, MELANIE LIDMAN and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As Trump administration reforms the EPA, cleanups of America's most toxic sites are uncertain
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just over a mile from where Patricia Flores has lived for almost 20 years, a battery smelter plant spewed toxic elements into the environment for nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Eastern U.S. braces for flooding while fire-damaged California city now faces mudslides
item.description: SIERRA MADRE, Calif. (AP) — Much of the Eastern U.S. braced for a renewed round of harsh, soggy weather on Saturday, with thunderstorms and melting snow combining to pound the Ohio and Tennessee valleys. The lower Mississippi valley was facing the threat of strong tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, ABC 4

item.title: Fearful immigrants ask Florida activist to sign guardianship papers for their children
item.description: Since President Donald Trump took office, immigrants in the U.S. illegally have changed their travel patterns and try to stay home, out of fear they could be deported.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: LOVELAND SKI AREA, Colo. (AP) — There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the crisp, frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Tigers, zebras and other stuffed animals will get new homes after health concerns kept them hidden
item.description: Crocodiles, monkeys, tigers, zebras and dozens of other taxidermy animals will move to new homes after concerns about arsenic exposure forced the closure of the South Dakota museum where they had been displayed for decades.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where US adults think the government is spending too much, according to AP-NORC polling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults believe the federal government is overspending — but polling also shows that many Americans, including Republicans, think the country is spending too little on major government programs such as Social Security.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Andrew Lester pleads guilty in shooting of unarmed Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl
item.description: Andrew Lester pleaded guilty to second-degree assault after shooting 16-year-old Ralph Yarl outside Kansas City, Missouri home in April 2023
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy will kick off his bid for Ohio governor in Cincinnati on Feb. 24
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Vivek Ramaswamy is preparing to launch his 2026 bid for Ohio governor on Feb. 24 in his native Cincinnati, with ensuing announcements held over two days in the Columbus, Toledo and Cleveland areas, The Associated Press has learned.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helicopter crew in collision with plane may not have heard key instruction from tower, NTSB says
item.description: Investigators say the crew of the Army helicopter that collided in midair with an American Airlines jet near Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Park Service removes transgender references from Stonewall National Monument website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: White House deputy chief of staff Dan Scavino posted a video Friday showing the frame up close and revealing it is the front page of the New York Post from Aug. 25, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department asks court to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: The Justice Department has asked a court to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Valentine’s Day dangers: Dating app killers lure love seekers in unsuspecting ways
item.description: While dating apps may be the perfect place to find lasting connections, sometimes finding love can turn into a deadly encounter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE deports migrant gang member who illegally entered US 10 times and is wanted for murder
item.description: A gang member who entered the U.S. illegally 10 times over the last 23 years and is wanted for murder in Mexico has been deported, Immigration and Customs Enforcement announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CBP officer pleads not guilty to migrant smuggling, drug conspiracy
item.description: A U.S. Customs and Border Protection officer charged with multiple counts of violating immigration and drug laws has pleaded not guilty to the charges, court documents show.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Six deadliest non-combat military aviation accidents in the last 5 years
item.description: A crash involving a U.S. Navy electronic-warfare aircraft in California this week is drawing attention to non-combat military aviation accidents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma governor says he'll stop plan to collect kids' immigration status
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt said he will stop a plan pushed by the state’s top education official to collect the immigration status of children when they register for public school.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alleged serial shoplifter banned from every Walmart in the USA
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A serial shoplifter has gotten herself banned from every Walmart in the United States of America due to her history of stealing from the company, Memphis Police say. Ashley Cross, 37, is charged with criminal trespass and theft of merchandise less than $1,000.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: In rare case, Georgia father jailed after his son was accused of a school shooting is granted bond
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — In a rare case of a parent being charged after a school shooting, a judge on Tuesday granted a $500,000 bond for the father of a 14-year-old boy accused of a deadly attack at a north Georgia high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
